verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, named after a Japanese marriage shrine, with documented roles in sperm-egg fusion. IZUMO family proteins (IZUMO1-4) are expressed predominantly in testis/sperm. IZUMO1 is the canonical member with a well-characterized single-pass TM domain and outer-leaflet surface display on sperm, critical for gamete fusion. IZUMO4 is the least-characterized family member but shares the IZUMO domain and is expressed in testis. Given its germline/gamete-restricted expression pattern and family-level surface biology, if it does reach the outer leaflet it does so only in the narrow context of sperm/germline cells. This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. No documented somatic cell-surface pool, no cancer ecto-display, no lysosomal exocytosis, no activation-induced display, and no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering outside germline is documented. The TM anchor status of IZUMO4 specifically is less clearly annotated than IZUMO1, adding uncertainty.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 TM domain presence and outer-leaflet topology in sperm is less well-documented than IZUMO1; the precise subcellular localization within sperm is uncertain.
